epics.title: Wire a repository-local audio harmony adapter

tasks.title: Add a non-LLM audio extraction adapter for mp3 input
acceptance_criteria: The music-analysis package can call a repository-local audio adapter for audio-file input.
The adapter returns beat-aligned chord candidates plus explicit uncertainty.
Adapter failures degrade into structured uncertainty instead of crashing the pipeline.
done_definition: Python analysis script is checked into the repository.
TypeScript adapter parses the Python output with schema validation.

tasks.title: Convert audio chord candidates into the normalized lead-sheet schema
acceptance_criteria: Audio chord candidates become measures and chord events in the normalized lead sheet.
Measure boundaries use a conservative beats-per-measure rule derived from the adapter meter hint.
Low-confidence or empty measures are marked for review.
done_definition: Audio pipeline emits structured sections, systems, measures, and evidence.
Uncertainty from parsing and segmentation is preserved.

tasks.title: Verify the adapter against the provided mp3 and project checks
acceptance_criteria: Lint completes without new blocking errors.
The provided Lady Gaga and Bruno Mars mp3 produces structured adapter output.
done_definition: Verification artifacts record the local checks and any remaining non-blocking risks.

assumptions: python3 plus librosa are available in the local environment even though ffmpeg is not.
A conservative beat-level audio draft is acceptable as long as uncertainty remains explicit.
risks: Beat and meter inference from audio alone can mis-detect slow pop ballads.
Template-matching chord recognition will miss richer chord qualities and some slash-bass motion.
